hyperedges.label: Six-Table PostgreSQL Schema (Raw SQL and Prisma Representations)
JWT Auth Token Lifecycle (cookies, CSRF, sessions)
Manual Onboarding Four-Step Flow (localStorage draft to single commit)
Skill Map Autosave Persistence Pipeline (reducer to debounced PUT to Prisma)

nodes.label: Analyze a session file and return token usage broken down by agent.
nodes.norm_label: analyze a session file and return token usage broken down by agent.

nodes.label: Format token count with thousands separators.
nodes.norm_label: format token count with thousands separators.

nodes.label: NOTE: This file should not be edited
nodes.norm_label: note: this file should not be edited

nodes.label: Principle I: Algorithm Correctness (SM-2)
nodes.rationale: SM-2 is the product's core value proposition; scheduling must be faithful: quality mapping Again=0/Hard=2/Good=3/Easy=5, ease factor floor 1.3, newInterval = interval * easeFactor, lifecycle phases enforced, learning steps with sub-day intervals, and validation against reference SM-2 outputs before merge.

nodes.label: Principle II: Offline-First
nodes.rationale: App must function fully without network; reviews never blocked by connectivity; sync is an enhancement layered on local-first storage with conflict resolution defined before sync work begins.

nodes.label: Principle III: Test-First
nodes.rationale: Tests before implementation for all scheduling logic and review state transitions; Red-Green-Refactor for algorithm code; SM-2 edge cases (ease floor, interval overflow, phase transitions) need dedicated tests; UI layout may be test-after.

nodes.label: Principle IV: Data Integrity
nodes.rationale: Card state and review history are the user's most valuable data: ReviewState mutations must be atomic, no silent corruption, schema changes require migrations, deletion is soft-delete or requires explicit confirmation with export option.

nodes.label: Principle V: Critical Path UX
nodes.rationale: The review flow (show front, think, reveal, rate) is the highest-priority interaction: <200ms load, instant card transitions, daily limits enforced to prevent burnout, four rating buttons always visible after reveal.

nodes.label: Principle VI: Simplicity
nodes.rationale: Minimum viable feature set; card types limited to Basic/Reverse/Cloze in v1; no plugins, scripting, or custom scheduling until the core review loop is stable; YAGNI on sync/import-export/statistics until the core cycle is validated.

nodes.label: Feature Spec: Spaced Repetition Backend Service
nodes.norm_label: feature spec: spaced repetition backend service

nodes.label: Card (Entity)
nodes.rationale: Three card types: Basic, Reverse, Cloze. Reverse cards auto-generate two separate Card records (front-to-back and back-to-front), each independently scheduled, matching Anki behavior and keeping scheduling logic uniform (Clarification Session 2026-03-19).

nodes.label: CardState / ReviewState (Entity)
nodes.rationale: Scheduling state kept separate from card content to allow independent evolution — interval, ease factor, repetitions, due date, lifecycle phase.

nodes.label: SM-2 Review Flow (US2)
nodes.rationale: The review loop is the core product experience — spaced repetition has no value without it. Due cards where dueDate <= now; ratings Again/Hard/Good/Easy update interval, ease factor (floor 1.3), repetitions, due date atomically; interval capped at 365 days.

nodes.label: Card Lifecycle Phases (US3)
nodes.rationale: Phases New -> Learning -> Review -> Relearning affect how cards are scheduled and presented, building on core review mechanics.

nodes.label: Daily Review Limits (US4)
nodes.rationale: Configurable limits (default 20 new / 200 reviews, UTC daily reset) prevent burnout and are a key differentiator for sustainable learning habits.

nodes.label: Study Statistics (US5)
nodes.rationale: Retention rate, reviews/day, time spent, accuracy — valuable for motivation and self-assessment but not essential for core learning (P3).

nodes.label: Implementation Plan: Spaced Repetition Backend
nodes.rationale: Single NestJS project with feature-based modules (decks, cards, reviews, statistics); database is a shared module with raw SQL via pg; SM-2 isolated as a pure service for testability. No auth in v1; atomic review state updates.

nodes.label: Decision R1: NestJS with Fastify Adapter
nodes.rationale: Fastify is ~2x faster than Express for JSON serialization; the high-frequency review endpoint benefits from schema-based serialization. Express rejected as slower; Hono/Elysia rejected for lacking NestJS integration.
nodes.norm_label: decision r1: nestjs with fastify adapter

nodes.label: Decision R2: Raw SQL with node-postgres (pg)
nodes.rationale: User requirement; raw SQL gives full control over query optimization for the due-cards date-filter query; Neon works natively with pg over SSL. TypeORM, Prisma, and Drizzle explicitly rejected. Migrations via numbered .sql files tracked in schema_migrations.
nodes.norm_label: decision r2: raw sql with node-postgres (pg)

nodes.label: Decision R3: SM-2 Algorithm Implementation
nodes.rationale: Classic SM-2: well-documented, deterministic, testable; constitution mandates it. EF' = EF + (0.1 - (5-q)*(0.08 + (5-q)*0.02)); default EF 2.5, min 1.3, max interval 365d; q<3 resets reps and interval=1; reps 0 -> 1d, reps 1 -> 6d, else interval*EF. FSRS rejected as too complex for v1 per Simplicity principle.

nodes.label: Decision R4: Neon Serverless PostgreSQL
nodes.rationale: User-provided infrastructure; standard PostgreSQL wire protocol works with pg over SSL; pooler endpoint in URL; keep pool max ~10 connections for serverless.

nodes.label: Decision R5: Reverse Card Strategy
nodes.rationale: Auto-generate two separate Card rows (original and swapped front/back), both type 'basic' after creation, linked via sourceCardId, each with its own CardState — matches Anki and requires no special-casing in review logic.

nodes.label: Decision R6: Card Lifecycle Phase Transitions
nodes.rationale: Graduation from Learning (and Relearning back to Review) requires one Good/Easy rating — simplest model matching Anki defaults; kept non-configurable per Simplicity principle, can be made configurable later.
nodes.norm_label: decision r6: card lifecycle phase transitions

nodes.label: Decision R7: Statistics Time Range
nodes.rationale: All-time by default with optional date-range filter — most useful default without overcomplicating the API.

nodes.label: cards table
nodes.rationale: GIN index on tags for cross-deck tag queries; partial index on deck_id excluding soft-deleted rows; source_card_id links reverse-generated pairs; cloze uses {{answer}} syntax in front.

nodes.label: card_states table
nodes.rationale: DB-level CHECK constraints enforce ease_factor >= 1.3 and 0 <= interval <= 365; due_date index serves the due-cards query.

nodes.label: daily_counters table
nodes.rationale: One row per UTC calendar day, upserted on each review to enforce daily limits.

nodes.label: REST API Contracts (/api/v1)
nodes.rationale: 14 endpoints across decks, cards, reviews, daily-limits settings, and statistics; review submission returns previous and new state; 429 when daily limit reached.

nodes.label: Tasks: Spaced Repetition Backend Service
nodes.rationale: 52 tasks in 8 phases grouped by user story; TDD mandatory for SM-2 (US2 tests not optional per constitution); US1 blocks US2; US3/US4/US5 parallel after US2.

nodes.label: Feature Spec: Migrate from Raw SQL to Prisma ORM
nodes.norm_label: feature spec: migrate from raw sql to prisma orm

nodes.label: Raw SQL to Prisma ORM Migration
nodes.rationale: Type-safe queries, auto-completion, single source of truth for the data model; zero behavioral regression required — all 14 endpoints must return identical responses and all tests pass unmodified; custom DatabaseService pool and migration runner removed.
nodes.norm_label: raw sql to prisma orm migration

nodes.label: Implementation Plan: Prisma Migration
nodes.rationale: 6 tables, 5 services, 14 endpoints to migrate; prisma/ dir at repo root; new src/prisma/ module wraps PrismaClient for NestJS DI; old src/database/ deleted after migration; Prisma judged to reduce complexity vs raw SQL strings per Simplicity.

nodes.label: Decision R1: PrismaService NestJS Integration Pattern
nodes.rationale: PrismaService extends PrismaClient with OnModuleInit ($connect), exported from a global PrismaModule via constructor DI — the official pattern, exactly replacing DatabaseService. nestjs-prisma package rejected as unnecessary wrapper; per-service instantiation rejected (no pooling/lifecycle).
nodes.norm_label: decision r1: prismaservice nestjs integration pattern

nodes.label: Decision R2: Prisma with Neon Serverless
nodes.rationale: Standard postgresql provider through Neon's pooler endpoint; @prisma/adapter-neon only needed for edge runtimes, not this Node.js TCP backend.
nodes.norm_label: decision r2: prisma with neon serverless

nodes.label: Decision R3: Introspect Existing DB (prisma db pull)
nodes.rationale: Introspection guarantees the Prisma schema exactly matches production, avoiding drift; then rename to PascalCase/camelCase with @@map/@map preserving actual table/column names. Writing schema from scratch rejected due to risk of subtle differences.
nodes.norm_label: decision r3: introspect existing db (prisma db pull)

nodes.label: Decision R4: Preserving PostgreSQL-Specific Features
nodes.rationale: GIN index via @@index([tags], type: Gin); CHECK constraints (ease_factor >= 1.3, interval 0-365) not expressible in Prisma schema — remain at DB level; native upsert() replaces raw ON CONFLICT; Prisma enums for card type and phase.

nodes.label: Decision R5: Interactive Transactions for Review Submission
nodes.rationale: prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => ...) gives the same ACID guarantees as the previous BEGIN/COMMIT pattern for the read-state -> compute-SM-2 -> update -> log -> increment-counter flow; batch transactions rejected (no reads inside), app-level locks rejected as unnecessary.
nodes.norm_label: decision r5: interactive transactions for review submission

nodes.label: Decision R6: Explicit Soft-Delete Filtering
nodes.rationale: Explicit where: { deletedAt: null } in every query touching decks/cards — Prisma middleware and prisma-extension-soft-delete rejected as hidden complexity/over-engineering for a small codebase.

nodes.label: Decision R7: Zero-Downtime Migration Baseline
nodes.rationale: prisma db pull then prisma migrate resolve --applied 'init' marks current DB state as baseline without running SQL against the existing database; future changes go through prisma migrate dev; old custom runner and .sql files deleted.

nodes.label: Prisma Schema (6 models, 1:1 with existing tables)
nodes.rationale: PascalCase models mapped via @@map to snake_case tables: Deck, Card (self-relation ReverseCards, GIN tags index), CardState (1:1 Card), ReviewLog (append-only), DailyLimit (singleton), DailyCounter (unique date, upsert). CHECK constraints stay at DB level as they are not expressible in Prisma schema.
nodes.norm_label: prisma schema (6 models, 1:1 with existing tables)

nodes.label: Tasks: Prisma Migration
nodes.rationale: 30 tasks in 6 phases; services rewritten one at a time for incremental testing; AppModule updated last; sm2.service.ts untouched (pure logic, no DB).

nodes.label: Feature Spec: Vite React Frontend
nodes.rationale: Simple SPA to exercise the backend: no auth, API base URL via env var, no complex state management library, lightweight styling — consistent with the single-user backend design.

nodes.label: Backend/Frontend Monorepo Restructure (US1, P0)
nodes.rationale: Structural prerequisite: existing backend moved into backend/, frontend scaffolded in frontend/ — all other work depends on this separation; backend must function identically after the move.

nodes.label: Review Session UI (US4)
nodes.rationale: Core learning loop in the UI: due cards shown one at a time, front first, reveal back, rate recall (Again/Hard/Good/Easy), rating submitted to backend.

nodes.label: Plan 003 (Unfilled speckit Template)
nodes.rationale: This plan.md was never filled in — it still contains the generic speckit plan template placeholders; the actual planning content for feature 003 lives in research.md, data-model.md, and tasks.md.

nodes.label: Decision R1: Two Independent package.json (No Monorepo Tool)
nodes.rationale: Simplest approach for two projects with no shared code; Turborepo/Nx overkill, npm workspaces add complexity for shared deps that don't exist yet.
nodes.norm_label: decision r1: two independent package.json (no monorepo tool)

nodes.label: Decision R2: Vite 6 + React 19 + React Router 7 + TailwindCSS 4
nodes.rationale: Vite for fastest dev server, React 19 latest stable and user-agreed, React Router 7 standard SPA routing, Tailwind 4 enables friendly design without a component library. CSS Modules, shadcn/ui, and Zustand/Redux rejected — React state + fetch is sufficient.
nodes.norm_label: decision r2: vite 6 + react 19 + react router 7 + tailwindcss 4

nodes.label: Decision R3: Thin Typed Fetch Wrapper (No axios/react-query)
nodes.rationale: ~10 API calls total; a typed fetch wrapper with error handling suffices. Axios adds a dependency for minimal benefit; React Query/SWR premature for simple read/write patterns; no OpenAPI spec so manual typing is fine.
nodes.norm_label: decision r3: thin typed fetch wrapper (no axios/react-query)

nodes.label: Decision R4: git mv Backend Restructure
nodes.rationale: git mv preserves history; config paths (tsconfig, nest-cli, prisma) updated for new location. Symlinks rejected as fragile; copy+delete loses history.
nodes.norm_label: decision r4: git mv backend restructure

nodes.label: Decision R5: Backend CORS for Frontend Dev Origin
nodes.rationale: Frontend (5173) and backend (3000) run on different ports; CORS enabled via NestJS/Fastify options. Vite proxy rejected because it masks the real deployment setup and CORS is needed in production anyway.
nodes.norm_label: decision r5: backend cors for frontend dev origin

nodes.label: Frontend API TypeScript Interfaces
nodes.rationale: No local data model or storage — TypeScript interfaces (Deck, Card, ReviewDueResponse, ReviewResult, Statistics, DailyLimits) mirror backend API response shapes; no caching layer in v1, simple fetch on mount/action.

nodes.label: Tasks: Vite React Frontend
nodes.rationale: 40 tasks in 7 phases; restructure first (Phase 1), then foundational API client/types/layout/routing, then deck CRUD MVP, cards, review session, stats; tests omitted as not explicitly requested.

nodes.label: Spec Quality Checklist: Anki-Like Learning Steps & Interval Hints
nodes.norm_label: spec quality checklist: anki-like learning steps & interval hints

nodes.label: Implementation Plan: Anki-Like Learning Steps & Interval Hints
nodes.norm_label: implementation plan: anki-like learning steps & interval hints

nodes.label: Feature Specification: Anki-Like Learning Steps & Interval Hints
nodes.norm_label: feature specification: anki-like learning steps & interval hints

nodes.label: Tasks: Anki-Like Learning Steps & Interval Hints
nodes.norm_label: tasks: anki-like learning steps & interval hints

nodes.label: Learning Steps
nodes.rationale: Core bug fix: 'Again' previously set interval to 1 day so failed cards never reappeared in-session. Anki-style sub-day steps ([1, 10] minutes) let failed cards cycle back within the same study session, which is the fundamental behavior users expect from Anki-style tools.

nodes.label: Interval Hints
nodes.rationale: Users need to see the consequence of each rating choice (e.g. Again: 1m, Good: 10m, Easy: 4d) to make informed decisions; matches Anki UX.

nodes.label: Learning Steps Config
nodes.rationale: Defaults: newSteps [1,10] min, relearningSteps [10] min, graduatingInterval 1440 min (1d), easyGraduatingInterval 5760 min (4d) — Anki's battle-tested defaults.

nodes.label: Minute-Based Interval Storage
nodes.rationale: Store intervals as integer minutes (1440 = 1 day): sub-day precision without schema type change; existing values migrated via interval * 1440. Rejected separate intervalMinutes field (splits logic) and float days (floating point imprecision causes scheduling bugs).

nodes.label: Server-Side Interval Hint Calculation
nodes.rationale: Hints computed in SM-2 service and returned in the due-cards response, keeping algorithm logic in one place. Rejected client-side calc (duplicates SM-2 logic) and per-card endpoint (N+1 requests).

nodes.label: Graduating Interval Defaults
nodes.rationale: Graduating interval 1 day (1440 min), Easy graduating interval 4 days (5760 min) — Anki's proven defaults matching user expectations.

nodes.label: Spec Quality Checklist: JWT Auth, Settings & Navigation
nodes.norm_label: spec quality checklist: jwt auth, settings & navigation

nodes.label: REST API Contract: Auth, Users & Sessions
nodes.norm_label: rest api contract: auth, users & sessions

nodes.label: Data Model: JWT Auth, Settings & Navigation
nodes.norm_label: data model: jwt auth, settings & navigation

nodes.label: Implementation Plan: JWT Auth, Settings & Navigation
nodes.norm_label: implementation plan: jwt auth, settings & navigation

nodes.label: Feature Specification: JWT Auth, Settings & Navigation
nodes.norm_label: feature specification: jwt auth, settings & navigation

nodes.label: JWT Authentication (email/password)
nodes.rationale: Foundation feature: without auth, no sessions, settings, or multi-user data isolation can work. Minimal v1: email/password only, no OAuth, no password reset; 15-min access token + 7-day refresh token.

nodes.label: Session Management (view/revoke)
nodes.rationale: Security: users need to know where they're logged in and revoke compromised sessions; current session cannot be revoked.

nodes.label: User Data Scoping via Deck
nodes.rationale: Only Deck gets a direct userId FK; Card, CardState, ReviewLog, DailyCounter inherit user scoping through their Deck relationship (card.deck.userId), avoiding redundant columns.

nodes.label: HttpOnly Cookie Token Storage
nodes.rationale: HttpOnly cookies are immune to XSS (localStorage rejected for XSS risk). Access token 15min SameSite=Strict; refresh token 7d path-scoped to /api/v1/auth/refresh. Uses @nestjs/jwt + passport-jwt with custom cookie extractor; @fastify/cookie required since backend runs Fastify, not Express.

nodes.label: CSRF Double-Submit Cookie Pattern
nodes.rationale: Because tokens live in auto-sent HttpOnly cookies, CSRF is a real threat on mutations. Stateless double-submit: non-HttpOnly csrf_token cookie mirrored in X-CSRF-Token header, validated by a guard on POST/PATCH/DELETE. Rejected synchronizer tokens (needs DB state), SameSite alone (insufficient), csurf (deprecated).

nodes.label: bcrypt Password Hashing (cost 12)
nodes.rationale: Industry standard, GPU-attack resistant; cost 12 balances security and ~250ms hash time. Argon2 rejected for weaker Node.js ecosystem support.

nodes.label: Server-Stored Theme Preference
nodes.rationale: Theme stored on User model so preference persists across devices; applied via CSS class on <html> with TailwindCSS darkMode:'class'; persisted via PATCH /users/me.

nodes.label: Destructive Auth Migration
nodes.rationale: Per clarification, all pre-auth data is wiped (truncate decks/cards/card_states/review_logs/daily_counters) before adding User/Session tables and userId FK — avoids assigning orphaned records to users.

nodes.label: Spec Quality Checklist: Next.js Fullstack Migration
nodes.norm_label: spec quality checklist: next.js fullstack migration

nodes.label: Implementation Plan: Migrate to Next.js Fullstack App
nodes.norm_label: implementation plan: migrate to next.js fullstack app

nodes.label: Research: Migrate to Next.js Fullstack App
nodes.norm_label: research: migrate to next.js fullstack app

nodes.label: Feature Specification: Migrate to Next.js Fullstack App
nodes.norm_label: feature specification: migrate to next.js fullstack app

nodes.label: Tasks: Migrate to Next.js Fullstack App
nodes.norm_label: tasks: migrate to next.js fullstack app

nodes.label: Next.js Fullstack Migration
nodes.rationale: Consolidate separate NestJS backend + Vite React frontend into one Next.js 15 App Router app with zero feature regression, same PostgreSQL/Prisma schema, single package.json and single dev command; old backend/ and frontend/ dirs deleted only after full verification (phased cleanup per clarification).

nodes.label: jose JWT + Next.js Middleware Auth
nodes.rationale: jose chosen because it is Edge Runtime compatible (jsonwebtoken is not); root middleware.ts checks auth cookies and protects routes. next-auth rejected as unnecessary abstraction over existing custom JWT auth; Server-Actions-only rejected for losing REST compatibility.

nodes.label: Route Handlers Replace NestJS Controllers
nodes.rationale: app/api/*/route.ts gives a 1:1 mapping to existing REST endpoints, minimizing behavioral change. tRPC rejected (client changes, overkill); Server-Actions-only rejected (no GET/external consumers); mixed approach rejected for migration complexity.

nodes.label: Zod Request Validation
nodes.rationale: Replaces NestJS class-validator DTOs: each DTO maps to a Zod schema with better TS inference and no decorator dependency. valibot rejected for weaker ecosystem.

nodes.label: Prisma Client Singleton Pattern
nodes.rationale: Next.js dev hot-reload creates multiple Prisma instances, so the client is cached on globalThis in development. Schema moves from backend/prisma/ to root prisma/ unchanged; Drizzle/Accelerate rejected.

nodes.label: Vitest + Playwright Testing Strategy
nodes.rationale: Vitest is Jest-API-compatible but faster with native ESM/TS, so SM-2 tests migrate with import-path changes only; Playwright covers full-stack e2e.

nodes.label: TanStack Query Migration + Silent Auth Refresh Plan
nodes.rationale: Replace ad-hoc useEffect+useState fetching and manual optimistic updates with TanStack Query v5, and fix the 15-minute logout bug by replacing reactive-only refresh with a proactive timer plus a module-level refresh lock that eliminates the race condition.
nodes.norm_label: tanstack query migration + silent auth refresh plan

nodes.label: Middleware Inline Refresh
nodes.rationale: On access-token verification failure for protected page routes, middleware.ts attempts an inline POST /api/auth/refresh with the refresh_token cookie and forwards Set-Cookie headers before falling back to a sign-in redirect. Applies only to page navigation; API routes rely on the client-side refresh lock.

nodes.label: QueryClient isServer Singleton Pattern
nodes.rationale: Module-level browserQueryClient singleton prevents the cache from being destroyed during React suspense transitions. useState(() => new QueryClient()) was rejected because suspense re-renders can recreate the client and wipe the cache.

nodes.label: Query Key Factory Pattern
nodes.rationale: Key factories (deckKeys.all, deckKeys.detail(id)) co-located with queryOptions() keep queryKey and queryFn in one place, preventing drift and enabling fuzzy invalidation. Inline per-component keys were rejected as fragile for invalidation.

nodes.label: Optimistic Updates Pattern (onMutate/onError/onSettled)
nodes.rationale: onMutate cancels queries, snapshots via getQueryData, applies optimistic state; onError rolls back from the context snapshot; onSettled always invalidates to sync with server truth. Update-on-confirm (wait for server) was rejected because it causes visible flicker on every mutation.

nodes.label: Module-Level Refresh Lock (refreshPromise)
nodes.rationale: Root cause of logouts: multiple concurrent requests all get 401, each independently calls /api/auth/refresh, causing refresh-token rotation conflicts. A refreshPromise singleton ensures only one refresh call is made; all callers await the same promise and retry with the new cookie.

nodes.label: Proactive Refresh at 80% Token Lifetime
nodes.rationale: Timer fires at 80% of token lifetime (12 of 15 min) using the token_expiry cookie, eliminating the 401 flash under normal usage; defers refresh while the tab is hidden. Longer token lifetime rejected as a security regression; reactive-only refresh rejected due to the race condition; interaction-only refresh rejected because background tabs lose sessions.

nodes.label: token_expiry Non-httpOnly Cookie
nodes.rationale: The access token is httpOnly and unreadable by JS, so the client needs a readable expiry timestamp to schedule proactive refresh. A plain timestamp is not a credential. localStorage rejected (not accessible in middleware); a readable duplicate token rejected as over-engineered.

nodes.label: useQuery over useSuspenseQuery Strategy
nodes.rationale: Standard useQuery with isPending/isError checks maps 1:1 to the existing useEffect+useState pattern. useSuspenseQuery removes the enabled option and requires Suspense + ErrorBoundary wrappers everywhere — a bigger refactor than needed, reserved for a future pass.

nodes.label: Auth Client Module (src/lib/auth-client.ts)
nodes.rationale: Extracted from use-auth.ts so fetchApi, the refresh lock, and getTokenExpiry are shareable between fetchApi and AuthProvider.

nodes.label: Onboarding Flow
nodes.rationale: Mandatory 4-step guided flow (language selection, AI level assessment chat, AI goal extraction chat, welcome) — the app cannot personalize content without knowing the user's language, level, and goals, so no protected route is reachable until onboardingCompleted is true.

nodes.label: User Onboarding Fields (onboardingCompleted / nativeLanguage / englishLevel / goals)
nodes.norm_label: user onboarding fields (onboardingcompleted / nativelanguage / englishlevel / goals)

nodes.label: Middleware onboardingCompleted Check
nodes.rationale: The access-token JWT carries onboardingCompleted so middleware can enforce the gate without a DB round-trip on every request; POST /api/onboarding/complete re-issues the token with onboardingCompleted: true.

nodes.label: Vercel AI Gateway with OIDC Auth
nodes.rationale: AI Gateway provides failover, cost tracking, OIDC auth (no raw API keys in env), and observability at <20ms overhead; plain 'provider/model' strings route automatically. Direct provider SDKs with raw API keys rejected because they bypass Gateway auth and observability.
nodes.norm_label: vercel ai gateway with oidc auth

nodes.label: AI SDK v6 Streaming Chat Route Pattern (streamText)
nodes.rationale: App Router route handler uses streamText with google/gemini-2.0-flash, convertToModelMessages, inline tool objects with inputSchema, and toUIMessageStreamResponse(); tool execute persists results to DB server-side.
nodes.norm_label: ai sdk v6 streaming chat route pattern (streamtext)

nodes.label: AI SDK v6 useChat Hook Pattern
nodes.rationale: v6 useChat from @ai-sdk/react with DefaultChatTransport, sendMessage({ text }), status values ready/submitted/streaming/error, and message parts arrays.
nodes.norm_label: ai sdk v6 usechat hook pattern

nodes.label: MediaRecorder Voice Input as Progressive Enhancement
nodes.rationale: Browser MediaRecorder captures audio/webm and POSTs the blob to /api/onboarding/transcribe; text input remains fully functional if MediaRecorder is unavailable. No WebSocket or audio streaming needed.
nodes.norm_label: mediarecorder voice input as progressive enhancement

nodes.label: CSS-Only Leo Float Animation
nodes.rationale: CSS @keyframes float only — no GSAP, no JS — applied to the next/image wrapper in LeoCharacter.tsx serving public/characterneutralsmile.png.

nodes.label: Onboarding Flow Implementation Plan
nodes.rationale: Offline-first constitution exception justified: onboarding is a one-time setup flow explicitly dependent on AI streaming, not part of the core review loop; AI chat complexity is the core product differentiator — a static form cannot assess level or extract goals with the same quality.

nodes.label: Custom LLM Tool-Calling Engine
nodes.rationale: Remove all AI SDK packages (ai, @ai-sdk/react, @ai-sdk/google, @ai-sdk/openai) and call an OpenAI-compatible chat-completions endpoint directly (OpenRouter default; swappable via LLM_BASE_URL/LLM_MODEL) with streaming, tool calling, and chat state implemented manually in TypeScript.

nodes.label: OpenAI-Compatible SSE Streaming with Tools
nodes.rationale: SSE with stream: true against /v1/chat/completions; tool calls arrive as delta.tool_calls JSON string fragments that must be concatenated across chunks; finish_reason 'tool_calls' vs 'stop' distinguishes tool invocation from normal completion.

nodes.label: Server-Side Tool-Call Loop
nodes.rationale: The loop runs entirely server-side within a single route handler request: detect tool_calls, execute the tool (DB write), append assistant + tool messages, call the LLM again until finish_reason 'stop'. Keeps the client a simple stream reader and avoids extra HTTP round trips; client-side loop rejected because it would expose LLM_API_KEY.

nodes.label: Plain-Text ReadableStream Response to Client
nodes.rationale: Route handlers return a Response with a ReadableStream body and Content-Type text/plain — no SSE framing needed since only one event type (text tokens) is sent; EventSource rejected because it does not support POST.

nodes.label: useLLMChat Custom Hook
nodes.rationale: Custom useState + fetch hook replacing useChat from @ai-sdk/react: manages messages, status (ready/streaming/error), and sendMessage which reads the response body ReadableStream and appends tokens to the last assistant message.

nodes.label: Plain JSON Schema Tool Definitions (No Zod)
nodes.rationale: Tools defined as plain TypeScript objects matching the OpenAI tools array schema, using JSON Schema directly instead of Zod, since the full pipeline is controlled in-house.
nodes.norm_label: plain json schema tool definitions (no zod)

nodes.label: ClientChatMessage
nodes.rationale: Simpler than server-side ChatMessage — no tool_calls or tool_call_id metadata is needed in browser state; the client only ever sees user and assistant text.

nodes.label: 009 Plan (Unfilled Template)
nodes.rationale: This plan.md is the raw speckit plan-template with placeholders — it was never filled in for feature 009; design intent lives in research.md and spec.md instead.

nodes.label: LLM Client Module (src/lib/llm.ts)
nodes.rationale: Single point of contact with the LLM API — callLLMStream, the streaming parser, the tool-call loop, and streamToClient live here; routes must not call fetch directly.

nodes.label: Manual Onboarding (no LLM)
nodes.rationale: Chat-based onboarding made ~12 LLM requests per signup, blowing through OpenRouter free-tier limits and turning expensive on paid models. Removing LLM from the first-contact path eliminates rate-limit flakes and per-user cost; median completion time drops from ~180s to under 90s.

nodes.label: 13-Item CEFR Level Scale (LevelChoice)
nodes.rationale: CEFR codes (A1..C2 solid, Fluent) are universally recognized, so chip values need no i18n; manual self-selection replaces the chat-based LLM level assessment. Stored as the exact chip string on users.englishLevel, validated server-side against the fixed 13-value whitelist.

nodes.label: GoalsPayload
nodes.rationale: JSON object {primary (required, <=120 chars), secondary?, context?, urgency? enum casual|moderate|urgent} stored on users.goals; optional fields omitted, not null. Replaces the chat-based goal extractor while preserving the same goals.primary field that drives tutor personalization.

nodes.label: OnboardingProgress (derived resume state)
nodes.rationale: Conceptual entity, no new DB table: the earliest incomplete User field determines the resume step. Completion order is fixed: language -> level -> goals -> flag-flip.

nodes.label: Dead-Code Cleanup of LLM Chat Stack
nodes.rationale: OnboardingChat, VoiceInput, useLLMChat, useOnboardingChat, src/lib/llm.ts, prompts.ts, copy.ts, and /api/onboarding/chat routes are deleted rather than kept 'just in case' — prevents accidental re-import regression. LLM_API_KEY/LLM_BASE_URL/LLM_MODEL stay in .env.example for future non-onboarding features.
nodes.norm_label: dead-code cleanup of llm chat stack

nodes.label: localStorage-First Onboarding with Single HTTP Commit
nodes.rationale: Verification plan confirming steps 1-3 write only to localStorage (no fetch, no DB reads) and step 4 makes exactly one POST /api/onboarding/complete persisting all three fields. Zero code changes required — all four invariants already hold. Noted out-of-scope: root page always redirects to step-1 (OB-1) and localStorage drafts persist indefinitely (OB-2).
nodes.norm_label: localstorage-first onboarding with single http commit

nodes.label: Onboarding Draft (readDraft/writeDraft/clearDraft)
nodes.rationale: Client draft in localStorage under KEY='onboardingDraft' via src/lib/onboarding/clientState.ts; each step pre-fills from and writes to the draft, never the server, until commit.

nodes.label: POST /api/onboarding/complete Atomic Commit
nodes.rationale: Single prisma.user.update writes nativeLanguage, englishLevel, goals, interfaceLanguage and flips onboardingCompleted=true in one DB call, then re-issues the JWT with the updated claim so middleware sees the flag on the next request.

nodes.label: i18n Short-Circuit to English on /onboarding/*
nodes.rationale: src/i18n/request.ts returns {locale:'en', messages:en.json} for any /onboarding/* path without touching Prisma — guarantees no hidden DB read of nativeLanguage for localizing onboarding steps.

nodes.label: LevelPicker Component
nodes.rationale: Client component rendering the 13 CEFR chips from LEVELS in a responsive grid (4x3+1 desktop, 2-column mobile), reusing existing onb-chip styling tokens.

nodes.label: GoalsForm Component
nodes.rationale: Required primary textarea with 120-char counter, optional secondary/context textareas, urgency as a three-option segmented control; empty optionals omitted from the payload (FR-006).

nodes.label: Skill Map (FigJam-style Hub Canvas)
nodes.rationale: Phase 1 is canvas hub only — no skill/exercise integration, no collaboration, no export, exactly one map per user. Infinite pan/zoom canvas with text/sticky/shape nodes, straight+orthogonal connectors, undo/redo, debounced per-user autosave.

nodes.label: Connector (straight/orthogonal, side-anchored)
nodes.rationale: Directed link anchored to a specific side on each end — moving nodes must never auto-reroute to a different side. Bend-handle drag axis is locked at drag start to prevent flipping or multiplying bends; endpoint drop on empty space reverts (no orphaned endpoints).

nodes.label: Undo/Redo History (80-deep, session-only)
nodes.rationale: Snapshots taken at action boundaries (create, delete, move-start, resize-start, port-drop, endpoint re-anchor, bend-drag-start), never per-frame during drags — per-frame snapshotting would make undo jump pixel-by-pixel and inflate memory. In-memory only, not persisted.

nodes.label: Viewport State (client-only pan/zoom)
nodes.rationale: Current pan + zoom {x,y,z}, z bounded [0.15, 4]. Client-only, not persisted server-side in Phase 1 — cross-device viewport restoration is explicitly not required.

nodes.label: Progress Sidebar Entry (Insights Group, NEW Badge)
nodes.rationale: Extends the existing AppLayout sidebar rather than adding a parallel nav abstraction; a floating dashboard entry point was rejected as less discoverable (SC-001/SC-008). NEW pill is a launch-period marker.
nodes.norm_label: progress sidebar entry (insights group, new badge)

nodes.label: Seeded Sample Map (first-visit content)
nodes.rationale: No first-time user ever sees a blank canvas: title, four skill cards (SPEAKING/LISTENING/READING/WRITING), stickies, text labels, and mixed straight+ortho connectors. Once any state is saved (including explicit empty) the seed never re-applies.

nodes.label: Implementation Plan: Skill Map Phase 1
nodes.rationale: Constitution check passes with one justified deviation: Offline-First (II) is waived because the Skill Map is an auxiliary server-persisted hub feature — local-only IndexedDB would lose the map on data clears and block device switching, while the offline-critical review flow is untouched.

nodes.label: DOM Nodes + Single SVG Overlay Rendering
nodes.rationale: Design handoff explicitly rejects react-flow (customization surface larger than rolling ~200 lines of plain geometry). Konva/Pixi/<canvas> rejected because they break text editing, accessibility, Tailwind reuse, and dark-mode theming. DOM nodes allow contentEditable; one SVG overlay shares the arrow marker and beats per-edge SVGs.
nodes.norm_label: dom nodes + single svg overlay rendering

nodes.label: useReducer + Snapshot Undo Stack
nodes.rationale: Single reducer owns {nodes, edges, selection, editing}; undo is a stack of shallow-copied snapshots capped at 80, taken at action boundaries. Immer judged overkill, zustand not already in the stack, DB-persisted history rejected because viewport + history are per-session.

nodes.label: Connector Geometry Pure Functions (portPoint/orthoRoute/nearestSideToPoint)
nodes.rationale: orthoRoute returns stable {axis:'h'|'v', value} segment descriptors derived solely from port sides, not rendered geometry — this fixes the handoff's axis-flip and duplicate-bend gotchas. segOverrides[i] is an idempotent scalar per segment, avoiding bend multiplication; storing bend paths as point arrays was rejected because it accumulates duplicates every frame.

nodes.label: TanStack Query Autosave with 500ms Debounce
nodes.rationale: 500ms balances 'save within 1s of last edit' (SC-004) against burst typing; per-action saves too chatty, save-on-blur loses changes on crashes, WebSocket out of Phase 1 scope. On failure: retry with backoff, local state stays source of truth. Reuses the app's existing TanStack Query v5 QueryClient.

nodes.label: Load-or-Seed GET (seed returned without writing)
nodes.rationale: GET returns the seeded sample when no row exists but never persists it — the first PUT materializes the row. This makes 'delete everything then reload' stay empty (FR-042). Seed-on-signup rejected (writes rows for users who never open the feature); seed-on-first-PUT rejected (more client branching).

nodes.label: SkillMap Prisma Model
nodes.rationale: Separate table (not a Json column on User) keyed by unique userId with delete-cascade: keeps User clean, enforces one-map-per-user at the DB, and evolves without rewriting User migrations. Nodes and edges live in Json columns; edge referential integrity is enforced in PUT validation, not DB FKs, because nodes are inside the blob.

nodes.label: SkillMapDoc Payload (Node/Edge discriminated unions)
nodes.rationale: {title 1..255, nodes <=2000, edges <=4000}. Node is a Zod-strict discriminated union on type (text/sticky/shape); Edge carries from/fromSide/to/toSide/kind plus optional per-segment scalar segOverrides. Orphan edge references, non-finite coordinates, and oversize strings are rejected with 422 and the prior row is untouched.

nodes.label: Client Doc Reducer State Transitions
nodes.rationale: Table of actions (createNode, updateNodeText, moveNodes, resizeNode, deleteSelection, createEdge, reanchorEdge, overrideSegment, setConnectorKind, undo/redo) with explicit snapshot boundaries. deleteSelection cascades edges referencing deleted nodes in the same action; undo stack caps at 80 with oldest dropped.

nodes.label: GET/PUT /api/map Contract
nodes.rationale: GET loads or returns the seed with isSeed:true without writing; PUT is a validated full-document replace (first PUT creates the row). 422 with Zod issues on invalid payload, prior row intact. Cache-Control: no-store; hard caps (2000/4000) are the only Phase-1 guardrail — autosave debounce keeps request rate under ~2 rps so no rate limiter is needed.

nodes.label: Quickstart — Skill Map Canvas (non-obvious rules)
nodes.rationale: Codifies eight gotchas: sticky ports never auto-reroute, ortho bend axis locked at mousedown from the stable segment descriptor, segOverrides[i] is a scalar, undo snapshots at action boundaries capped at 80, empty state means no server row (never re-seed), edit-mode mousedown must not start a drag, delete cascades edges, endpoint drop on empty space reverts.

nodes.label: Task List: Skill Map Phase 1
nodes.rationale: 48 tasks across 10 phases; Constitution III (Test-First) applies to pure logic (geometry, Zod, reducer, API contract) with UI interactions test-after. US1 alone (sidebar + read-only seeded canvas) is a shippable MVP.

nodes.label: Specification Quality Checklist (Skill Map)
nodes.rationale: Validation passed on first pass with zero NEEDS CLARIFICATION markers; all content-quality, requirement-completeness, and feature-readiness items checked.

nodes.label: FairyTales Page (Streaming Story Generation)
nodes.rationale: Experimental local-only feature on the existing empty /fairy-tales protected page: character form (up to 15 name+description rows) + tale brief, Generate button streams Gemini 2.0 Flash (via OpenRouter) chunk-by-chunk into the page. Constraints: no commits/pushes, no new npm deps, hardcoded English strings (skip useTranslations to avoid i18n parity tests), dev server on port 3001.

nodes.label: streamLLMCompletion SSE Helper
nodes.rationale: New src/lib/llmStream.ts: streaming variant of callLLMCompletion. OpenRouter is OpenAI-compatible, so streaming is just stream:true plus SSE parsing — yield choices[0].delta.content per data: line, stop on [DONE]. Mirrors src/lib/llm.ts env vars (LLM_BASE_URL, LLM_MODEL, LLM_API_KEY) and reuses its LLMMessage type.

nodes.label: Tale Brief Framework (Setting/Premise/Conflict/Tone)
nodes.rationale: Minimal 4-field brief derived from the classic screenwriting premise/logline structure (setting -> conflict -> tone -> theme), with plain-English labels. Only premise is required; combined with the dynamic character list it drives the system+user prompt.

nodes.label: POST /api/fairy-tales/generate Streaming Route
nodes.rationale: requireAuth first, Zod-validated body (1..15 characters, premise required), returns a ReadableStream of raw text chunks with Content-Type text/plain and Cache-Control no-cache; forwards request.signal so client AbortController cancellation propagates to OpenRouter; error sentinel chunk on mid-stream failure.

hyperedges.label: Six-Table PostgreSQL Schema (Raw SQL and Prisma Representations)
JWT Auth Token Lifecycle (cookies, CSRF, sessions)
Manual Onboarding Four-Step Flow (localStorage draft to single commit)
Skill Map Autosave Persistence Pipeline (reducer to debounced PUT to Prisma)
